description: 1 Esdras is a Greek recomposition of material found in the Hebrew Bible: 2 Chronicles 34-36, Ezra 1-10, and Nehemiah 7:73-8:13. The alignment below maps each 1 Esdras chapter:verse range to its Hebrew parallel (if any). Verses 3:1-5:6 (the 'Story of the Three Youths' / Zerubbabel's triumph at Darius's court) have NO Hebrew parallel -- they are the unique Greek material that distinguishes 1 Esdras. Chapter 1 parallels 2 Chronicles 35-36. Chapters 2, 5:7-end, 6-9:36 parallel Ezra 1-10. Chapter 9:37-55 parallels Nehemiah 7:73-8:13.
source_note: Alignment follows the standard table given in NETS (New English Translation of the Septuagint), Talshir's commentary (Atlanta: SBL, 2001), and the Göttingen LXX apparatus (Hanhart 1974). Where there is disagreement on boundaries between sources, the NETS boundaries are preferred.
hebrew_witness: WLC (Westminster Leningrad Codex) -- already in cartha-translation corpus under sources/ot/
notes: Numbering: 1 Esdras follows the LXX Esdras A chapter/verse system (Hanhart edition). The MT parallel columns use standard Hebrew Bible chapter/verse numbering.
1 Esdras has no Hebrew original -- it was composed in Greek, though it incorporates material that was later (and independently) translated from Hebrew into LXX 2 Esdras (= Ezra-Nehemiah).
The 'Story of the Three Youths' (3:1-5:6) is widely regarded as originally a Persian tale adapted into this composition. No Hebrew/Aramaic Vorlage is known.
Where 1 Esdras diverges structurally from MT (e.g. placement of Ezra 4:7-24 earlier in the narrative), the mapping follows the 1 Esdras order, not the MT order.

alignment.content: Summary of Josiah's reign -- unique to 1 Esdras (possibly expanded from 2 Chr 35:20a)
Last kings of Judah; fall of Jerusalem
Cyrus's decree; return of temple vessels
Complaint to Artaxerxes; work on temple halted (relocated earlier in the narrative)
Story of the Three Youths / Zerubbabel at Darius's court -- UNIQUE TO 1 ESDRAS. No Hebrew parallel. This is the 'added' material that makes 1 Esdras distinct from canonical Ezra.
Rebuilding the altar; foundation of the temple; opposition
Haggai and Zechariah; Darius's decree; completion and dedication of the temple
Ezra's genealogy and commission from Artaxerxes
List of those returning with Ezra; arrival in Jerusalem
Dissolution of mixed marriages; list of offenders
Ezra reads the Law to the assembly. NOTE: 1 Esdras ends here; the rest of Nehemiah 8 and all of Nehemiah 9-13 have no 1 Esdras parallel.

unique_to_1esdras.summary: The 'Story of the Three Youths' (3:1-5:6) is the single largest block without Hebrew parallel. It is an independent composition that frames Zerubbabel's return as the outcome of a wisdom contest at Darius's court. For translation-phase work, this section must be translated from Greek alone (Swete/Rahlfs), since there is no Hebrew witness to consult.

material_in_mt_not_in_1esdras: Nehemiah 8:13b-18 (continuation of the Law reading, Feast of Booths)
Nehemiah 9-13 (covenant renewal, list of signatories, dedication of the wall, final reforms)
